hardware.name: ESP32
hardware.description: Dual-core Wi-Fi & Bluetooth microcontroller. Ideal for most IoT projects.
hardware.bestFor: Smart home, sensor hubs, BLE devices

hardware.name: Arduino Uno
hardware.description: Classic open-source microcontroller board. Best for learning and prototyping.

hardware.name: Raspberry Pi 4
hardware.description: Full Linux computer on a single board. Runs Python, Node.js, and more.
hardware.bestFor: Edge computing, ML inference, media server

hardware.name: STM32F4
hardware.description: High-performance ARM Cortex-M4 MCU. Used in industrial and real-time systems.
hardware.bestFor: Industrial control, real-time systems, motor control

hardware.name: Arduino Nano 33 IoT
hardware.description: Compact Arduino with built-in Wi-Fi and BLE. Perfect for wearables.
hardware.bestFor: Wearables, compact IoT nodes, portable sensors

hardware.name: NVIDIA Jetson Nano
hardware.description: AI and computer vision at the edge. Runs TensorFlow and PyTorch.

protocols.name: MQTT
protocols.description: Lightweight publish-subscribe protocol. Ideal for constrained devices and high-latency networks.

protocols.name: HTTP/REST
protocols.description: Standard web protocol. Easy to integrate with web apps and cloud APIs.
protocols.bestFor: Cloud APIs, web dashboards, one-time data uploads

protocols.name: CoAP
protocols.description: UDP-based protocol designed for constrained environments. REST-like simplicity at low power.

protocols.name: WebSocket
protocols.description: Full-duplex communication over a single TCP connection. Great for live dashboards.
protocols.bestFor: Live dashboards, real-time monitoring, bidirectional control

protocols.name: Zigbee
protocols.description: Low-power mesh networking protocol for short-range IoT. Used in smart home devices.
protocols.bestFor: Smart home mesh, building automation, battery-powered sensors

protocols.name: LoRaWAN
protocols.description: Long-range, low-power wide area network. Perfect for remote outdoor deployments.
protocols.bestFor: Agriculture, smart city, asset tracking over large areas

cloud.name: AWS IoT Core
cloud.description: Scalable cloud platform for connecting billions of IoT devices. Full integration with AWS ecosystem.
cloud.bestFor: Enterprise scale, complex workflows, AWS-native apps

cloud.name: Google Cloud IoT
cloud.description: Managed IoT platform with BigQuery and ML integration. Excellent for data analytics.

cloud.name: Azure IoT Hub
cloud.description: Microsoft's fully managed IoT hub with device management and security features.

cloud.name: ThingSpeak
cloud.description: Simple IoT analytics platform. Easy to use with Arduino and MATLAB visualization.

cloud.name: Blynk
cloud.description: No-code IoT platform with drag-and-drop mobile dashboard. Very fast prototyping.

cloud.name: Mosquitto (Self-hosted)
cloud.description: Open-source MQTT broker you can host yourself. Full control, no cloud dependency.

software.name: Node-RED
software.description: Low-code programming tool for wiring IoT flows visually. Drag-and-drop integration.

software.name: MicroPython
software.description: Python implementation optimized for microcontrollers (ESP32, Raspberry Pi Pico).

software.name: FreeRTOS
software.description: Real-time operating system for microcontrollers. Manages tasks, timers, and memory.

software.name: PlatformIO
software.description: Professional IDE and build system for embedded systems. Supports 40+ platforms.
software.useCase: Professional firmware development, CI/CD for embedded

software.name: InfluxDB
software.description: Time-series database optimized for IoT sensor data. Used with Grafana for dashboards.
software.useCase: Storing sensor readings, trend analysis, alerts

software.name: Grafana
software.description: Open-source analytics and monitoring platform. Creates beautiful IoT dashboards.
software.useCase: Live monitoring dashboards, sensor data visualization
